# Other regulatory notes

## BOI / Corporate Transparency Act

As of the FinCEN materials updated in March 2025, domestic entities created in the United States are exempt from BOI reporting under the revised rule.

Official source:

- FinCEN FAQ PDF: https://www.fincen.gov/system/files/shared/BOI-FAQs-QA-508C.pdf

This can change again, so re-check before filing or relying on it.

## California sales tax is separate

State income-tax exemption is not the same thing as sales-tax exemption.

Official FTB reminder:

- FTB 3500A instructions: https://www.ftb.ca.gov/forms/misc/3500A-instructions.html

That page explicitly says not to use `FTB 3500A` for California sales-tax exemptions and directs organizations to CDTFA.

Practical meaning:

- a 501(c)(3) is not automatically exempt from California sales/use tax in all contexts.

## Property tax

Property-tax relief is a separate analysis, often through the welfare exemption framework if the organization owns and uses qualifying property.

Official BOE resources start here:

- https://www.boe.ca.gov/

## Multistate fundraising

If `gecon.ai` solicits donations outside California, charitable registration rules in other states may apply even if the organization is California-based.

This becomes relevant once fundraising is national rather than local/institutional.
